source_docs.title: HyDrop enables droplet-based single-cell ATAC-seq and single-cell RNA-seq using dissolvable hydrogel beads
HyDrop enables droplet-based single-cell ATAC-seq and single-cell RNA-seq using dissolvable hydrogel beads

oligos.name: Acrydite primer (unbarcoded bead PCR handle)
oligos.role: Acrydite/disulfide-anchored PCR-handle primer copolymerized into the hydrogel bead; the split-pool barcode oligos anneal and extend from here
oligos.notes: 5' Acrydite + internal disulfide (iThioMC6-D) for polymerization and reductive (DTT) release. Contains a T7 promoter (AATACGACTCACTATAGGG) and the PCR handle AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC.

oligos.name: HyDrop-ATAC barcoded bead primer (final, 117 bp)
oligos.role: Fully barcoded capture primer on the dissolvable bead; 3' end is complementary to the Tn5 transposon adapter and captures/indexes tagmented chromatin

components.name: 5' Acrydite + disulfide linker
components.role: polymerization anchor + reductive release

components.name: Spacer + T7 promoter + PCR handle
components.role: constant PCR handle / read region

components.name: Cell barcode BC1
components.role: split-pool barcode round 1 (1 of 96)

components.name: Barcode linker 1
components.role: constant spacer

components.name: Cell barcode BC2
components.role: split-pool barcode round 2 (1 of 96)

components.name: Barcode linker 2
components.role: constant spacer

components.name: Cell barcode BC3
components.role: split-pool barcode round 3 (1 of 96)

components.name: ATAC capture / Nextera N7 (S7) handle
components.role: complementary to Tn5 transposon adapter

oligos.notes: 884,736 (96^3) possible barcodes. Concrete example bead in Supplementary file 1: BC1=TTCCTGTGAG, BC2=TCGGACTTAT, BC3=GGCTGAATTA. The 30 bp read barcode = BC1+BC2+BC3.

oligos.name: Sub-barcode primer round 1 (BC1)
oligos.role: Split-pool linear-amplification template that anneals to the PCR handle and appends barcode round 1 + linker 1 (1 of 96)

components.name: Linker-1 complement
components.role: templates the CAGCTACTGC linker onto the bead

components.name: Barcode-1 template
components.role: reverse complement of one of 96 BC1 barcodes

components.name: PCR-handle anneal
components.role: anneals to the 3' end of the bead PCR handle

oligos.name: Sub-barcode primer round 2 (BC2)
oligos.role: Split-pool linear-amplification template that anneals to linker 1 and appends barcode round 2 + linker 2 (1 of 96)

components.name: Linker-2 complement
components.role: templates the CGAGTACCCT linker onto the bead

components.name: Barcode-2 template
components.role: reverse complement of one of 96 BC2 barcodes

components.name: Linker-1 anneal
components.role: anneals to linker 1 (CAGCTACTGC) on the bead

oligos.name: Sub-barcode primer round 3 — ATAC (BC3 + ATAC capture)
oligos.role: Final split-pool template that anneals to linker 2 and appends barcode round 3 + the ATAC (Tn5-adapter-complementary) capture sequence (1 of 96)

components.name: ATAC capture complement
components.role: templates the GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG ATAC capture handle onto the bead

components.name: Barcode-3 template
components.role: reverse complement of one of 96 BC3 barcodes

components.name: Linker-2 anneal
components.role: anneals to linker 2 (CGAGTACCCT) on the bead

oligos.notes: 96 variants (ATAC version). Example: CCGAGCCCACGAGACTGACCGTACTAGGGTACTCG. The RNA version instead appends UMI + poly(dT).

oligos.name: HyDrop i7 library primer (HYi7)
oligos.role: Bulk-PCR primer adding P7 and the i7 sample index; anneals to the bead PCR handle. i7 index read is sacrificed to read the cell barcode.

components.name: P7
components.role: P7 flow-cell adapter

components.name: i7 sample index
components.role: 10 bp i7 index (16 provided; not used for demultiplexing)

components.name: Linker
components.role: constant spacer

components.name: PCR handle
components.role: anneals to bead PCR handle; 3' phosphorothioate (*A*C)

oligos.name: HyDrop-ATAC i5 library primer (HYi5)
oligos.role: Bulk-PCR primer adding P5 and the i5 sample index; anneals to the gap-filled Tn5 (S5) adapter

components.name: P5
components.role: P5 flow-cell adapter

components.name: i5 sample index
components.role: 10 bp i5 index used for sample demultiplexing

components.name: Nextera S5 (Read 1) handle
components.role: anneals to the Tn5 read-1-side adapter

oligos.notes: 16 indexed variants, e.g. HYi5_1 i5=TCGTGGAGCG. A phosphorylated variant (HYi5_ATAC_P, 3' *T*G) is provided but not used.

oligos.name: Tn5 mosaic end (ME)
oligos.role: 19 bp Tn5 recognition/mosaic-end sequence inserted at both ends of every tagmented fragment

oligos.name: Tn5 transposon adapter (barcode-capture / Read 2 side)
oligos.role: Custom Tn5 adapter whose N7/S7 handle (GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG) is complementary to the bead ATAC capture sequence, enabling in-droplet barcode capture

components.name: Adapter top (S7 handle + ME)
components.role: transferred strand

components.name: Adapter bottom (ME complement)
components.role: non-transferred mosaic-end strand

oligos.name: Read 1 sequencing primer (Nextera S5 + ME)
oligos.role: Sequences genomic ATAC paired-end mate 1 from the i5/S5 (P5-proximal) transposon end
oligos.notes: Constructed from the S5 handle in HYi5 (TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTG) + the mosaic end (AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG).

oligos.name: Read 2 sequencing primer (Nextera S7 + ME)
oligos.role: Sequences genomic ATAC paired-end mate 2 from the i7-proximal transposon end
oligos.notes: Same S7/ATAC-capture handle + mosaic end. The 30 bp cell barcode is read as index 1 from this side (standard i7 index-read primer or a HyDrop custom primer).

oligos.name: Anti-Acrydite FAM QC probe
oligos.role: FISH QC probe against the acrydite PCR-handle stub on unbarcoded beads (baseline fluorescence)

oligos.name: Anti-ATAC FAM QC probe
oligos.role: FISH QC probe against the ATAC capture site on fully barcoded ATAC beads

oligos.name: Anti-BC1 FAM QC probe
oligos.role: FISH QC probe against one of 96 BC1 sub-barcodes

oligos.name: Anti-BC2 FAM QC probe
oligos.role: FISH QC probe against one of 96 BC2 sub-barcodes

oligos.name: Anti-BC3 FAM QC probe
oligos.role: FISH QC probe against one of 96 BC3 sub-barcodes

final_library.source_label: HyDrop-ATAC (De Rop et al., eLife 2022; Supplementary file 2)
annotation_lines: [SAMPLE_INDEX:10] = i7 sample index (not used for demultiplexing)
AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC = PCR handle / barcode read region
GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG = Nextera Read 2 (S7) / ATAC capture handle

evidence.locator: HyDrop-ATAC (De Rop et al., eLife 2022; Supplementary file 2)

library_generation.title: Bulk Tn5 tagmentation of nuclei
library_generation.summary: Accessible chromatin in bulk nuclei is fragmented and tagged with custom Tn5 transposon adapters.
library_generation.note: Nuclei (Pitstop-permeabilized) are tagmented in bulk with a custom Tn5 that inserts asymmetric mosaic-end adapters; one side carries the GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG (ATAC-capture / S7) handle.
library_generation.product:
5'- GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG XXXXX...XXXXX CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT -3'
3'-                TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC XXXXX...XXXXX GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGACTGCGACGGCTGCT -5'

library_generation.title: Droplet encapsulation, bead dissolution & gap fill
library_generation.summary: Tagmented nuclei are co-encapsulated with a dissolvable barcoded bead; DTT releases primers and thermocycling gap-fills the 9 bp ME gap.
library_generation.note: In-droplet: DTT dissolves the bead and releases the barcoded primer; heating denatures Tn5 and a strand-displacing polymerase fills the mosaic-end gaps, producing a fully duplex, adapter-flanked fragment.
library_generation.product:
5'- GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG XXX...XXX CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGCTGCCGACGA -3'
3'- CAGAGCACCCGAGCCTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC XXX...XXX GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGACTGCGACGGCTGCT -5'

library_generation.title: Barcode capture (bead primer anneals to fragment)
library_generation.summary: The bead primer's 3' ATAC-capture end anneals to the S7 handle on the tagmented fragment.
library_generation.note: The 3' end of the barcoded bead primer (…GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG) hybridizes to the bottom-strand CAGAGCACCCGAGCC of the gap-filled fragment, priming linear copy of the fragment.

library_generation.title: In-droplet linear amplification (13 cycles)
library_generation.summary: Linear PCR off the bead primer produces multiple barcoded copies of each genomic fragment.
library_generation.note: 13 cycles of linear amplification append the full cell barcode + PCR handle to the accessible-chromatin fragment; only the bead-primer strand is amplified.

library_generation.title: Emulsion breaking, pooling & cleanup
library_generation.summary: Droplets are broken (PFO/GITC) and the barcoded single-strand products are pooled and bead-purified.
library_generation.note: Recovery agent + GITC + DTT break the emulsion; Dynabeads and Ampure purification recover the pooled, cell-indexed ATAC fragments.

library_generation.title: Bulk indexing PCR (P5/P7)
library_generation.summary: HYi7 and HYi5 primers add P7 + i7 index and P5 + i5 index to complete the sequenceable library.
library_generation.note: 12–13 cycles of KAPA HiFi PCR with HYi7 (P7 + i7, anneals to PCR handle) and HYi5 (P5 + i5, anneals to the S5 Tn5 adapter); double-sided Ampure size selection yields the final library.

library_sequencing.primer: Read 1 sequencing primer (Nextera S5 + ME: TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG)
library_sequencing.note: ATAC paired-end mate 1 — genomic insert read from the i5/S5 (P5-proximal) transposon end. Primer anneals the top strand; its 3' end sits at the ME/insert junction and synthesis runs leftward into the accessible-chromatin fragment.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- XXXX...XXXX CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGCTGCCGACGA [i5] GTGTAGATCTCGGTGGTCGCCGTATCATT -3'   (library top)
3'- XXXX...XXXX GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGACTGCGACGGCTGCT [i5] CACATCTAGAGCCACCAGCGGCATAGTAA -5'   (library bottom)
   <------- reads insert   3'-GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGACTGCGACGGCTGCT-5'  Read 1 primer (S5+ME), anneals top strand

library_sequencing.primer: i7 index-read primer / HyDrop_CustSeq (anneals top strand at the S7/ATAC-capture handle GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG)
library_sequencing.note: The i7 index read is sacrificed to read the 30 bp HyDrop cell barcode. Primer anneals the S7 handle on the top strand; 3' end points into BC3 and synthesis runs leftward through BC3-linker-BC2-linker-BC1 (~50 bp).
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- ...AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC[BC1]CAGCTACTGC[BC2]CGAGTACCCT[BC3]GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG... -3'   (library top)
3'- ...TTCGTCACCATAGTTGCGTCTCATG[BC1]GTCGATGACG[BC2]GCTCATGGGA[BC3]CAGAGCACCCGAGCC... -5'   (library bottom)
   <------- reads BC3-linker-BC2-linker-BC1      3'-CAGAGCACCCGAGCC-5'  Index 1 primer, anneals top strand at S7

library_sequencing.primer: i5 index-read primer (anneals bottom strand at the S5 handle GACGCTGCCGACGA)
library_sequencing.note: 10 bp i5 sample index used for library/sample demultiplexing. Primer anneals the bottom strand at the S5 handle; 3' end points into the i5 and synthesis runs rightward across it.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- ...CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGACGCTGCCGACGA [i5] GTGTAGATCTCGGTGGTCGCCGTATCATT -3'   (library top)
3'- ...GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGACTGCGACGGCTGCT [i5] CACATCTAGAGCCACCAGCGGCATAGTAA -5'   (library bottom)
   Index 2 primer: 5'-GACGCTGCCGACGA------->  reads i5   (anneals bottom strand)

library_sequencing.primer: Read 2 sequencing primer (Nextera S7 + ME: GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG)
library_sequencing.note: ATAC paired-end mate 2 — genomic insert read from the i7 (S7/ATAC-capture) transposon end. Primer is identical to the top-strand S7+ME, so it anneals the bottom strand; 3' end sits at the ME/insert junction and synthesis runs rightward into the fragment.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- ...[BC3]GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG XXXX...XXXX -3'   (library top)
3'- ...[BC3]CAGAGCACCCGAGCCTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC XXXX...XXXX -5'   (library bottom)
   Read 2 primer (S7+ME): 5'-GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG------->  reads insert (anneals bottom strand)

title: HyDrop-ATAC
description: HyDrop-ATAC is an open-source, droplet-microfluidic single-cell ATAC-seq assay that uses dissolvable, barcoded polyacrylamide hydrogel beads. Nuclei are Tn5-tagmented in bulk with a custom transposon, then co-encapsulated with a dissolvable bead carrying a 96×96×96 split-pool cell barcode ending in a sequence complementary to the Tn5 adapter. Inside the droplet the bead dissolves (DTT-triggered), the Tn5 is denatured, and the accessible-chromatin fragments are gap-filled, captured, and cell-indexed by linear amplification off the bead primer. Pooled fragments are then bulk-PCR amplified with P5/P7 index primers. Sequencing reads the two genomic (ATAC) mates plus a 30 bp cell barcode (read as index 1) and a sample index (index 2).

reference.label: HyDrop enables droplet-based single-cell ATAC-seq and single-cell RNA-seq using dissolvable hydrogel beads

original_publication.title: HyDrop enables droplet-based single-cell ATAC-seq and single-cell RNA-seq using dissolvable hydrogel beads

throughput.summary: 7,996 high-quality single-nuclei chromatin accessibility profiles from flash-frozen mouse cortex in a single run; ~65% cell recovery with >90% bead occupancy.
throughput.cells: 7,996 nuclei (mouse cortex, single run); mixed-species runs recovered 1,353–3,068 cells
throughput.dna: median 4,148 unique fragments/cell (mouse cortex; median TSS enrichment 13, ~53% FRiP); 2,705 unique fragments/cell in species-mixing

publication.statistical_model: cisTopic Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) topic modeling of the single-cell region-accessibility matrix; droplet loading leverages beating-Poisson close-packed bead encapsulation

other.label: Barcode whitelist matching
other.value: ~88% of barcode reads matched at 1 mismatch across the 30 bp barcode

other.label: Instruments
other.value: Illumina NextSeq500 / NextSeq2000; Droplet Genomics Onyx microfluidics

data_processing.summary: Barcode reads are adapter-trimmed and error-corrected against the 884,736-member whitelist (≤1 mismatch); the two ATAC mates are aligned with bwa-mem, PCR-deduplicated with samtools, and converted to a fragments file with Sinto via the VSN scATAC-seq pipeline. Cells are filtered on unique-fragment count, TSS enrichment, and Scrublet, then cisTopic LDA topic modeling drives Leiden clustering, gene-activity imputation/annotation, and Wilcoxon differential-accessibility calling.
data_processing.statistical_model: cisTopic Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) topic modeling; differential accessibility by one-vs-all Wilcoxon rank-sum test
